steps.title: Set up the missing leg
steps.content:
Let $b$ be the other leg of the right triangle. Use the Pythagorean theorem with hypotenuse $262.4$ and one leg $57.6$.
$$57.6^2+b^2=262.4^2$$

steps.title: Solve for the leg
steps.content:
Move the known leg square to the other side and take the positive square root.
$$b^2=262.4^2-57.6^2$$
$$b^2=68{,}853.76-3{,}317.76=65{,}536$$
$$b=256$$
The answer is C.
